title: harnessed stack
description: A composition of one harness + a chosen set of recipes (catalog/stacks/<agent>_<recipe>…/stack.yaml). Nothing is resolved at runtime; recipes are assembled ahead of time. See docs/harnessed-design.md §5/§12.

name.description: Stack identity. Convention: <agent>_<recipe>[_<recipe>…] (underscores between fields; hyphens allowed within a name).

harness.description: The single harness this stack targets. Exactly one per stack (never two at once). Defaults to claude.

permissions.description: Permission mode written into the profile. 'prompt' (default) asks before tool use; 'yolo' writes per-harness skip-permission config — safe in an isolated instance.

recipes.description: Recipe names composed onto the harness, in declaration order. Each must resolve in the catalog (user overlay first, then repo).

services.description: Shared services attached by reference. Started host-published, idempotently, outliving the instance.

state.description: State and lifecycle options. Default persistent; `--fresh` overrides at runtime.

persist.description: Keep state across runs (default true). `--fresh` at runtime forces a throwaway volume.

session_state.description: Where Claude session state (projects/ + history.jsonl) lives. 'host' (default) persists to a harnessed-owned host dir, inspectable; 'volume' uses a throwaway per-instance volume.
